No Poverty.reason: Course discusses taxation, which can influence poverty reduction policies.

Quality Education.reason: Course aims to familiarize students with taxation, relevant for economic education.
evidence: Let students be familiar with advanced especially dynamic taxation

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Taxation theories can support economic growth and decent work policies.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Taxation can address inequalities through fiscal policies.

No Poverty.reason: The study of "theories of taxation" and "policy analysis" is relevant to creating pro-poor policy frameworks (Target 1.b) and funding social protection systems (Target 1.3).

Good Health and Well-being.reason: "Theories of taxation" inform policies for health financing (Target 3.c) and specific taxes on products like tobacco (Target 3.a) to promote public health.

Quality Education.reason: Taxation is the primary funding mechanism for public education systems. Understanding "theories of taxation" is foundational to financing quality education (Target 4.1).

Gender Equality.reason: "Theories of taxation" can be applied to design gender-sensitive fiscal policies (Target 5.c) and social protection systems, though this is not explicitly stated.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: Taxation provides public funds for water infrastructure. Environmental taxation theories could relate to reducing water pollution (Target 6.3).

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: "Theories of taxation" are directly applicable to creating fiscal incentives, like carbon taxes, to promote clean energy and investment (Targets 7.2, 7.a).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The "Economics of Taxation" directly informs development-oriented fiscal policies that support economic growth (Target 8.1) and productive activities (Target 8.3).
evidence: 財經專業與政策分析能力

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Tax policy, a core topic in "Economics of Taxation," is a key tool for funding infrastructure (Target 9.1) and incentivizing industrial innovation (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: A central theme in "Economics of Taxation" is designing fiscal policies (Target 10.4) to redistribute income and progressively achieve greater equality.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: "Theories of taxation" apply to municipal finance, which funds sustainable urban transport (Target 11.2) and other public services, though this is an indirect link.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course directly relates to Target 12.c, which calls for "restructuring taxation" to reflect environmental impacts, a core topic in "theories of taxation".

Climate Action.reason: "Theories of taxation" are fundamental to designing carbon taxes, a key method to "integrate climate change measures into national policies" (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: Tax and subsidy policies, part of "Economics of Taxation," can be used to address marine pollution (Target 14.1) and harmful fisheries subsidies (Target 14.6).

Life on Land.reason: "Theories of taxation" can inform policies to mobilize resources for conservation (Target 15.a) and integrate ecosystem values into national planning (Target 15.9).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: "Theories of taxation" are crucial for developing effective and transparent institutions (Target 16.6) and combating illicit financial flows (Target 16.4).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is a core concept for Target 17.1, which explicitly focuses on strengthening "domestic capacity for tax and other revenue collection" through understanding taxation.
